title: Explicitly request the release of container's resources and trigger post-stop hooks
body:
Extend the Container API with "Delete", and make use of it on interactive "podman run" sessions, so when they terminate the container's resources are released and the post-stop hooks run.

Not doing this may cause the OCI runtime to keep holding some resources until the container is removed. This is very visible with Kata, as the QEMU process and its proxy will keep running until "delete" is called for the runtime.

comments.body:
Can one of the admins verify this patch?
I understand the following commands:
- `bot, add author to whitelist`
- `bot, test pull request`
- `bot, test pull request once`

Hm. Maybe we should make delete() part of Cleanup(), which would not
require any changes to run and would also handle detached containers.

I like the delete API, though. Full review coming in a few hours.

On Tue, Sep 18, 2018, 05:19 Atomic Bot <notifications@github.com> wrote:

> Can one of the admins verify this patch?
> I understand the following commands:
>

>    - bot, test pull request
>    - bot, test pull request once

Yes lets do this in the podman container cleanup code.

> Hm. Maybe we should make delete() part of Cleanup(), which would not require any changes to run and would also handle detached containers. I like the delete API, though. Full review coming in a few hours.

I considered this option too, but reading the code I got the feeling that Cleanup() was intended just to clean up network and mountpoints. I'm fine with both approaches though.

@slp We want to extend cleanup to make sure that the container is fully cleaned up.  We also want to extend it to record the exit code for the container in to the libpod database.

podman container cleanup is called when every container exits, so we don't have to worry about handling containers in run as well as start and containers that are run in daemon mode.
@slp BTW I am glad someone is playing around with podman and Kata Containers...
I think you should add the Delete method and then have cleanup cal it.

> @slp BTW I am glad someone is playing around with podman and Kata Containers...

The combination of podman + Kata is amazing. It'd be even better if the latter supported labeling the shim, proxy and QEMU processes, as an additional security layer, but I guess they'll eventually get there.
@slp I'm going to poke at this over the weekend - I think I know the proper way forward, but it might take a bit of work to implement.
@slp Opened #1531 which should address the problems with restart
@mheon Ack, closing this one (next time I'll open an issue instead of a PR).
@slp Thanks for the contribution anyways - this issue ended up growing a lot of unexpected complexity under the hood.
@slp We like PR's and Issues.  Either way thanks for contributing.
